notes_md:
**tới** = to arrive, to reach a place or time. It’s a very common everyday verb.

Use it with places or events you physically arrive at: *tới nhà, tới công ty, tới bữa tiệc*. It also works for time and limits, meaning “reach” (a point in time or number).

It can mean “up to” or “until” in certain phrases.


Close synonym: **đến** (very similar; often interchangeable).
exampleSentencesTranslated: When you arrive at the station, the train has already left.
I will arrive at the office at eight o'clock tomorrow morning.
She texted her friend when she had just arrive at school.
notes:
tới = to arrive, to reach a place or time. It’s a very common everyday verb.

Use it with places or events you physically arrive at: tới nhà, tới công ty, tới bữa tiệc. It also works for time and limits, meaning “reach” (a point in time or number).

It can mean “up to” or “until” in certain phrases.

Close synonym: đến (very similar; often interchangeable).

example.en: I arrive at school at 7 o'clock.
example.es: Llego a la escuela a las 7 en punto.

definitions.en: to arrive; to come to a place.

exampleSentences: Khi em tới nhà ga, tàu đã rời đi mất rồi.
Anh sẽ tới văn phòng vào lúc tám giờ sáng mai.
Chị ấy nhắn tin cho bạn khi chị ấy vừa mới tới trường.
